content.text: I’ll inspect the compiler types, concurrency helpers, conclusion job generation, schema, and docs to understand the existing configuration flow.

content.content:
1	package workflow
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
8	)
9	
10	var concurrencyLog = logger.New("workflow:concurrency")
11	
12	// GenerateConcurrencyConfig generates the concurrency configuration for a workflow
13	// based on its trigger types and characteristics.
14	func GenerateConcurrencyConfig(workflowData *WorkflowData, isCommandTrigger bool) string {
15		concurrencyLog.Printf("Generating concurrency config: isCommandTrigger=%v", isCommandTrigger)
16	
17		// Don't override if already set
18		if workflowData.Concurrency != "" {
19			concurrencyLog.Print("Using existing concurrency configuration from workflow data")
20			return workflowData.Concurrency
21		}
22	
23		// Build concurrency group keys using the original workflow-specific logic
24		keys := buildConcurrencyGroupKeys(workflowData, isCommandTrigger)
25		groupValue := strings.Join(keys, "-")
26		concurrencyLog.Printf("Built concurrency group: %s", groupValue)
27	
28		// Build the concurrency configuration
29		concurrencyConfig := fmt.Sprintf("concurrency:\n  group: \"%s\"", groupValue)
30	
31		// Add cancel-in-progress if appropriate
32		if shouldEnableCancelInProgress(workflowData, isCommandTrigger) {
33			concurrencyLog.Print("Enabling cancel-in-progress for concurrency group")
34			concurrencyConfig += "\n  cancel-in-progress: true"
35		}
36	
37		return concurrencyConfig
38	}
39	
40	// GenerateJobConcurrencyConfig generates the agent concurrency configuration
41	// for the agent job based on engine.concurrency field
42	func GenerateJobConcurrencyConfig(workflowData *WorkflowData) string {
43		concurrencyLog.Print("Generating job-level concurrency config")
44	
45		// If concurrency is explicitly configured in engine, use it
46		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Concurrency != "" {
47			concurrencyLog.Print("Using engine-configured concurrency")
48			return workflowData.EngineConfig.Concurrency
49		}
50	
51		// Check if this workflow has special trigger handling (issues, PRs, discussions, push, command,
52		// or workflow_dispatch-only). For these cases, no default concurrency should be applied at agent level
53		if hasSpecialTriggers(workflowData) {
54			concurrencyLog.Print("Workflow has special triggers, skipping default job concurrency")
55			return ""
56		}
57	
58		// For remaining generic triggers like schedule, apply default concurrency
59		// Pattern: gh-aw-{engine-id}-${{ github.workflow }}
60		engineID := ""
61		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.ID != "" {
62			engineID = workflowData.EngineConfig.ID
63		}
64	
65		if engineID == "" {
66			// If no engine ID is available, skip default concurrency
67			return ""
68		}
69	
70		// Build the default concurrency configuration
71		groupValue := fmt.Sprintf("gh-aw-%s-${{ github.workflow }}", engineID)
72		// If the user specified a job-discriminator, append it so that concurrent
73		// runs with different inputs (fan-out pattern) do not share the same group.
74		if workflowData.ConcurrencyJobDiscriminator != "" {
75			concurrencyLog.Printf("Appending job discriminator to job-level concurrency group: %s", workflowData.ConcurrencyJobDiscriminator)
76			groupValue = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", groupValue, workflowData.ConcurrencyJobDiscriminator)
77		}
78		concurrencyConfig := fmt.Sprintf("concurrency:\n  group: \"%s\"", groupValue)
79	
80		return concurrencyConfig
81	}
82	
83	// hasSpecialTriggers checks if the workflow has special trigger types that require
84	// workflow-level concurrency handling (issues, PRs, discussions, push, command,
85	// slash_command, or workflow_dispatch-only)
86	func hasSpecialTriggers(workflowData *WorkflowData) bool {
87		on := workflowData.On
88		return isIssueWorkflow(on) ||
89			isPullRequestWorkflow(on) ||
90			isDiscussionWorkflow(on) ||
91			isPushWorkflow(on) ||
92			isSlashCommandWorkflow(on) ||
93			// workflow_dispatch-only represents explicit user intent — top-level concurrency is sufficient
94			isWorkflowDispatchOnly(on)
95	}
96	
97	// isPullRequestWorkflow checks if a workflow's "on" section contains pull_request triggers
98	func isPullRequestWorkflow(on string) bool {
99		return strings.Contains(on, "pull_request")
100	}
101	
102	// isIssueWorkflow checks if a workflow's "on" section contains issue-related triggers
103	func isIssueWorkflow(on string) bool {
104		return strings.Contains(on, "issues") || strings.Contains(on, "issue_comment")
105	}
106	
107	// isDiscussionWorkflow checks if a workflow's "on" section contains discussion-related triggers
108	func isDiscussionWorkflow(on string) bool {
109		return strings.Contains(on, "discussion")
110	}
111	
112	// isWorkflowDispatchOnly returns true when workflow_dispatch is the only trigger in the
113	// "on" section, indicating the workflow is always started by explicit user intent.
114	// It handles both rendered YAML (standard GitHub Actions events) and input YAML
115	// (which may contain synthetic events like slash_command before they are expanded).
116	func isWorkflowDispatchOnly(on string) bool {
117		if !strings.Contains(on, "workflow_dispatch") {
118			return false
119		}
120		// If any other common trigger is present as a YAML key, this is not a
121		// workflow_dispatch-only workflow. We check for the trigger name followed by
122		// ':' (YAML key in object form) or as the sole inline value to avoid false
123		// matches from input parameter names (e.g., "push_branch" ≠ "push" trigger).
124		// slash_command is included here because it is a synthetic event that expands
125		// to issue_comment + workflow_dispatch at compile time; its presence means the
126		// workflow is not triggered solely by explicit user dispatch.
127		otherTriggers := []string{
128			"push", "pull_request", "pull_request_review", "pull_request_review_comment",
129			"pull_request_target", "issues", "issue_comment", "discussion",
130			"discussion_comment", "schedule", "repository_dispatch", "workflow_run",
131			"create", "delete", "release", "deployment", "fork", "gollum",
132			"label", "milestone", "page_build", "public", "registry_package",
133			"status", "watch", "merge_group", "check_run", "check_suite",
134			"slash_command",
135		}
136		for _, trigger := range otherTriggers {
137			// Trigger in object format: "push:" / "  push:"
138			if strings.Contains(on, trigger+":") {
139				return false
140			}
141			// Trigger in inline format: "on: push" (no colon, trigger is the last token)
142			if strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(on), " "+trigger) {
143				return false
144			}
145		}
146		return true
147	}
148	
149	// isPushWorkflow checks if a workflow's "on" section contains push triggers
150	func isPushWorkflow(on string) bool {
151		return strings.Contains(on, "push")
152	}
153	
154	// isSlashCommandWorkflow checks if a workflow's "on" section contains the slash_command
155	// synthetic trigger. slash_command is an input-level event that expands to
156	// issue_comment + workflow_dispatch at compile time. Detecting it here allows
157	// the concurrency helpers to produce correct results even when they are called
158	// with the pre-rendered "on" YAML (before the event expansion has taken place).
159	func isSlashCommandWorkflow(on string) bool {
160		return strings.Contains(on, "slash_command")
161	}
162	
163	// entityConcurrencyKey builds a ${{ ... }} concurrency-group expression for entity-number
164	// based workflows. primaryParts are the event-number identifiers (e.g.,
165	// "github.event.pull_request.number"), tailParts are the trailing fallbacks (e.g.,
166	// "github.ref", "github.run_id"). When hasItemNumber is true, "inputs.item_number" is
167	// inserted between the primary identifiers and the tail, providing a stable per-item
168	// key for manual workflow_dispatch runs triggered via the label trigger shorthand.
169	func entityConcurrencyKey(primaryParts []string, tailParts []string, hasItemNumber bool) string {
170		parts := make([]string, 0, len(primaryParts)+len(tailParts)+1)
171		parts = append(parts, primaryParts...)
172		if hasItemNumber {
173			parts = append(parts, "inputs.item_number")
174		}
175		parts = append(parts, tailParts...)
176		return "${{ " + strings.Join(parts, " || ") + " }}"
177	}
178	
179	// botIsolatedConcurrencyKey builds a ${{ ... }} concurrency-group expression that
180	// routes GitHub App bot-triggered runs to a unique github.run_id key, preventing
181	// passive bot-authored comment events from colliding with the primary run's group.
182	// When contains(github.actor, '[bot]') is true, the expression short-circuits to
183	// github.run_id so that bot-triggered runs never share a group with human runs.
184	func botIsolatedConcurrencyKey(primaryParts []string, tailParts []string, hasItemNumber bool) string {
185		parts := make([]string, 0, len(primaryParts)+len(tailParts)+2)
186		// Prepend the bot-actor isolation check: bot runs always get a unique key
187		parts = append(parts, "contains(github.actor, '[bot]') && github.run_id")
188		parts = append(parts, primaryParts...)
189		if hasItemNumber {
190			parts = append(parts, "inputs.item_number")
191		}
192		parts = append(parts, tailParts...)
193		return "${{ " + strings.Join(parts, " || ") + " }}"
194	}
195	
196	// hasIssueCommentTrigger returns true when the workflow has any trigger that uses
197	// issue_comment events: explicit issue_comment, slash_command, or command triggers.
198	// These are the only triggers where a GitHub App-authored comment on an issue can
199	// re-enter the same workflow and match the primary run's workflow-level concurrency
200	// group, creating the self-cancellation risk described in the issue.
201	func hasIssueCommentTrigger(workflowData *WorkflowData) bool {
202		on := workflowData.On
203		return strings.Contains(on, "issue_comment") ||
204			strings.Contains(on, "slash_command") ||
205			len(workflowData.Command) > 0
206	}
207	
208	// hasBotSelfCancelRisk returns true when the workflow's auto-generated concurrency
209	// configuration can be collided with by a passive GitHub App bot-authored event.
210	// The dangerous combination is: issue_comment triggers + GitHub App safe-outputs.
211	// When this combination is present, App-authored comments posted by safe-outputs can
212	// re-trigger the workflow and resolve to the same workflow-level concurrency group
213	// as the originating run, causing cancel-in-progress to cancel the original run.
214	func hasBotSelfCancelRisk(workflowData *WorkflowData) bool {
215		if workflowData.SafeOutputs == nil || workflowData.SafeOutputs.GitHubApp == nil {
216			return false
217		}
218		return hasIssueCommentTrigger(workflowData)
219	}
220	
221	// buildConcurrencyGroupKeys builds an array of keys for the concurrency group
222	func buildConcurrencyGroupKeys(workflowData *WorkflowData, isCommandTrigger bool) []string {
223		keys := []string{"gh-aw", "${{ github.workflow }}"}
224	
225		// Whether this workflow exposes inputs.item_number via workflow_dispatch (label trigger shorthand).
226		// When true, include it in the concurrency key so that manual dispatches for different items
227		// use distinct groups and don't cancel each other.
228		hasItemNumber := workflowData.HasDispatchItemNumber
229	
230		// Detect whether the workflow is at risk of bot-self-cancellation.
231		// When safe-outputs uses a GitHub App token AND the workflow has issue_comment triggers,
232		// App-authored comments can re-trigger the workflow and collide with the primary run's
233		// concurrency group. When this risk is present we use botIsolatedConcurrencyKey so that
234		// bot-triggered runs always resolve to a unique github.run_id key instead.
235		botRisk := hasBotSelfCancelRisk(workflowData)
236		if botRisk {
237			concurrencyLog.Print("Bot self-cancel risk detected: applying bot-actor isolation to concurrency group key")
238		}
239	
240		// entityKey selects the correct concurrency key builder based on bot risk.
241		// It captures both botRisk and hasItemNumber from the outer scope, so call
242		// sites only need to supply the entity-specific parts.
243		entityKey := func(primaryParts []string, tailParts []string) string {
244			if botRisk {
245				return botIsolatedConcurrencyKey(primaryParts, tailParts, hasItemNumber)
246			}
247			return entityConcurrencyKey(primaryParts, tailParts, hasItemNumber)
248		}
249	
250		if isCommandTrigger || isSlashCommandWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
251			// For command/slash_command workflows: use issue/PR number; fall back to run_id when
252			// neither is available (e.g. manual workflow_dispatch of the outer workflow).
253			// When bot risk is detected, prepend the bot-actor isolation check.
254			if botRisk {
255				keys = append(keys, "${{ contains(github.actor, '[bot]') && github.run_id || github.event.issue.number || github.event.pull_request.number || github.run_id }}")
256			} else {
257				keys = append(keys, "${{ github.event.issue.number || github.event.pull_request.number || github.run_id }}")
258			}
259		} else if isPullRequestWorkflow(workflowData.On) && isIssueWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
260			// Mixed workflows with both issue and PR triggers
261			keys = append(keys, entityKey(
262				[]string{"github.event.issue.number", "github.event.pull_request.number"},
263				[]string{"github.run_id"},
264			))
265		} else if isPullRequestWorkflow(workflowData.On) && isDiscussionWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
266			// Mixed workflows with PR and discussion triggers
267			keys = append(keys, entityConcurrencyKey(
268				[]string{"github.event.pull_request.number", "github.event.discussion.number"},
269				[]string{"github.run_id"},
270				hasItemNumber,
271			))
272		} else if isIssueWorkflow(workflowData.On) && isDiscussionWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
273			// Mixed workflows with issue and discussion triggers
274			keys = append(keys, entityKey(
275				[]string{"github.event.issue.number", "github.event.discussion.number"},
276				[]string{"github.run_id"},
277			))
278		} else if isPullRequestWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
279			// PR workflows: use PR number, fall back to ref then run_id
280			keys = append(keys, entityConcurrencyKey(
281				[]string{"github.event.pull_request.number"},
282				[]string{"github.ref", "github.run_id"},
283				hasItemNumber,
284			))
285		} else if isIssueWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
286			// Issue workflows: run_id is the fallback when no issue context is available
287			// (e.g. when a mixed-trigger workflow is started via workflow_dispatch).
288			keys = append(keys, entityKey(
289				[]string{"github.event.issue.number"},
290				[]string{"github.run_id"},
291			))
292		} else if isDiscussionWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
293			// Discussion workflows: run_id is the fallback when no discussion context is available.
294			keys = append(keys, entityConcurrencyKey(
295				[]string{"github.event.discussion.number"},
296				[]string{"github.run_id"},
297				hasItemNumber,
298			))
299		} else if isPushWorkflow(workflowData.On) {
300			// Push workflows: use ref to differentiate between branches
301			keys = append(keys, "${{ github.ref || github.run_id }}")
302		}
303	
304		// For label-triggered workflows (label trigger shorthand or label_command), include
305		// github.event.label.name as an additional segment so that runs triggered by different
306		// labels do not share a concurrency group. Without this, adding multiple labels to the
307		// same PR/issue simultaneously (which fires one labeled event per label) would cause all
308		// matching workflow runs to share a group, and with cancel-in-progress enabled the last
309		// surviving run could be for a different label than the workflow expects.
310		if hasItemNumber {
311			keys = append(keys, "${{ github.event.label.name || github.run_id }}")
312		}
313	
314		return keys
315	}
316	
317	// shouldEnableCancelInProgress determines if cancel-in-progress should be enabled
318	func shouldEnableCancelInProgress(workflowData *WorkflowData, isCommandTrigger bool) bool {
319		// Never enable cancellation for command workflows
320		if isCommandTrigger {
321			return false
322		}
323	
324		// Enable cancellation for pull request workflows (including mixed workflows)
325		return isPullRequestWorkflow(workflowData.On)
326	}
327

1	---
2	title: Concurrency Control
3	description: Complete guide to concurrency control in GitHub Agentic Workflows, including agent job concurrency configuration and engine isolation.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 1400
6	---
7	
8	GitHub Agentic Workflows uses dual-level concurrency control to prevent resource exhaustion and ensure predictable execution:
9	- **Per-workflow**: Limits based on workflow name and trigger context (issue, PR, branch)
10	- **Per-engine**: Limits AI execution across all workflows via `engine.concurrency`
11	
12	## Per-Workflow Concurrency
13	
14	Workflow-level concurrency groups include the workflow name plus context-specific identifiers:
15	
16	| Trigger Type | Concurrency Group | Cancel In Progress |
17	|--------------|-------------------|-------------------|
18	| Issues | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ issue.number }}` | No |
19	| Pull Requests | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ pr.number \|\| ref }}` | Yes (new commits cancel outdated runs) |
20	| Push | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}` | No |
21	| Schedule/Other | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}` | No |
22	| Label-triggered (label trigger shorthand or label_command) | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ entity.number }}-${{ github.event.label.name }}` | Yes for PRs, No otherwise |
23	
24	This ensures workflows on different issues, PRs, or branches run concurrently without interference.
25	
26	For label-triggered workflows, the concurrency group includes `github.event.label.name` as an additional segment. This prevents cross-label cancellation when multiple labels are added to the same PR or issue simultaneously: each label event gets its own distinct group, so workflows triggered by different labels do not cancel each other.
27	
28	## Per-Engine Concurrency
29	
30	The default per-engine pattern `gh-aw-{engine-id}` ensures only one agent job runs per engine across all workflows, preventing AI resource exhaustion. The group includes only the engine ID and `gh-aw-` prefix - workflow name, issue/PR numbers, and branches are excluded.
31	
32	```yaml wrap
33	jobs:
34	  agent:
35	    concurrency:
36	      group: "gh-aw-{engine-id}"
37	```
38	
39	## Custom Concurrency
40	
41	Override either level independently:
42	
43	```yaml wrap
44	---
45	on: push
46	concurrency:  # Workflow-level
47	  group: custom-group-${{ github.ref }}
48	  cancel-in-progress: true
49	engine:
50	  id: copilot
51	  concurrency:  # Engine-level
52	    group: "gh-aw-copilot-${{ github.workflow }}"
53	tools:
54	  github:
55	    allowed: [list_issues]
56	---
57	```
58	
59	## Safe Outputs Job Concurrency
60	
61	The `safe_outputs` job runs independently from the agent job and can process outputs concurrently across workflow runs. Use `safe-outputs.concurrency-group` to serialize access when needed:
62	
63	```yaml wrap
64	safe-outputs:
65	  concurrency-group: "safe-outputs-${{ github.repository }}"
66	  create-issue:
67	```
68	
69	When set, the `safe_outputs` job uses `cancel-in-progress: false` — meaning queued runs wait for the in-progress run to finish rather than being cancelled. This is useful for workflows that create issues or pull requests where duplicate operations would be undesirable.
70	
71	See [Safe Outputs](/gh-aw/reference/safe-outputs/#safe-outputs-job-concurrency-concurrency-group) for details.
72	
73	## Conclusion Job Concurrency
74	
75	The `conclusion` job — which handles reporting and post-agent cleanup — automatically receives a workflow-specific concurrency group derived from the workflow filename:
76	
77	```yaml wrap
78	conclusion:
79	  concurrency:
80	    group: "gh-aw-conclusion-my-workflow"
81	    cancel-in-progress: false
82	```
83	
84	This prevents conclusion jobs from colliding when multiple agents run the same workflow concurrently. The group uses `cancel-in-progress: false` so queued conclusion runs complete in order rather than being discarded.
85	
86	This concurrency group is set automatically during compilation and requires no manual configuration.
87	
88	## Fan-Out Concurrency (`job-discriminator`)
89	
90	When multiple workflow instances are dispatched concurrently with different inputs (fan-out pattern), compiler-generated job-level concurrency groups are static across all runs — causing all but the latest dispatched run to be cancelled as they compete for the same slot.
91	
92	Use `concurrency.job-discriminator` to append a unique expression to compiler-generated job-level concurrency groups (`agent` and `output` jobs), making each dispatched run's group distinct:
93	
94	```yaml wrap
95	concurrency:
96	  job-discriminator: ${{ inputs.finding_id }}
97	```
98	
99	This generates a unique job-level concurrency group per dispatched run, preventing fan-out cancellations while preserving the per-workflow concurrency group at the workflow level.
100	
101	Example usage:
102	
103	```yaml wrap
104	concurrency:
105	  job-discriminator: ${{ inputs.finding_id }}
106	```
107	
108	Common expressions:
109	
110	| Scenario | Expression |
111	|---|---|
112	| Fan-out by a specific input | `${{ inputs.finding_id }}` |
113	| Universal uniqueness (e.g. scheduled runs) | `${{ github.run_id }}` |
114	| Dispatched or scheduled fallback | `${{ inputs.organization \|\| github.run_id }}` |
115	
116	:::note
117	`job-discriminator` is a gh-aw extension and is stripped from the compiled lock file. It does not appear in the generated GitHub Actions YAML.
118	:::
119	
120	:::note
121	`job-discriminator` has no effect on workflows triggered by `workflow_dispatch`-only, `push`, or `pull_request` events, or when the engine provides an explicit job-level concurrency configuration.
122	:::
123	
124	## Related Documentation
125	
126	- [AI Engines](/gh-aw/reference/engines/) - Engine configuration and capabilities
127	- [Frontmatter](/gh-aw/reference/frontmatter/) - Complete frontmatter reference
128	- [Workflow Structure](/gh-aw/reference/workflow-structure/) - Overall workflow organization
129	- [Safe Outputs](/gh-aw/reference/safe-outputs/) - Safe output processing and job configuration
130

---
title: Concurrency Control
description: Complete guide to concurrency control in GitHub Agentic Workflows, including agent job concurrency configuration and engine isolation.
sidebar:
  order: 1400
---

GitHub Agentic Workflows uses dual-level concurrency control to prevent resource exhaustion and ensure predictable execution:
- **Per-workflow**: Limits based on workflow name and trigger context (issue, PR, branch)
- **Per-engine**: Limits AI execution across all workflows via `engine.concurrency`

## Per-Workflow Concurrency

Workflow-level concurrency groups include the workflow name plus context-specific identifiers:

| Trigger Type | Concurrency Group | Cancel In Progress |
|--------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Issues | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ issue.number }}` | No |
| Pull Requests | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ pr.number \|\| ref }}` | Yes (new commits cancel outdated runs) |
| Push | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}` | No |
| Schedule/Other | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}` | No |
| Label-triggered (label trigger shorthand or label_command) | `gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ entity.number }}-${{ github.event.label.name }}` | Yes for PRs, No otherwise |

This ensures workflows on different issues, PRs, or branches run concurrently without interference.

For label-triggered workflows, the concurrency group includes `github.event.label.name` as an additional segment. This prevents cross-label cancellation when multiple labels are added to the same PR or issue simultaneously: each label event gets its own distinct group, so workflows triggered by different labels do not cancel each other.

## Per-Engine Concurrency

The default per-engine pattern `gh-aw-{engine-id}` ensures only one agent job runs per engine across all workflows, preventing AI resource exhaustion. The group includes only the engine ID and `gh-aw-` prefix - workflow name, issue/PR numbers, and branches are excluded.

```yaml wrap
jobs:
  agent:
    concurrency:
      group: "gh-aw-{engine-id}"
```

## Custom Concurrency

Override either level independently:

```yaml wrap
---
on: push
concurrency:  # Workflow-level
  group: custom-group-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
engine:
  id: copilot
  concurrency:  # Engine-level
    group: "gh-aw-copilot-${{ github.workflow }}"
tools:
  github:
    allowed: [list_issues]
---
```

## Safe Outputs Job Concurrency

The `safe_outputs` job runs independently from the agent job and can process outputs concurrently across workflow runs. Use `safe-outputs.concurrency-group` to serialize access when needed:

```yaml wrap
safe-outputs:
  concurrency-group: "safe-outputs-${{ github.repository }}"
  create-issue:
```

When set, the `safe_outputs` job uses `cancel-in-progress: false` — meaning queued runs wait for the in-progress run to finish rather than being cancelled. This is useful for workflows that create issues or pull requests where duplicate operations would be undesirable.

See [Safe Outputs](/gh-aw/reference/safe-outputs/#safe-outputs-job-concurrency-concurrency-group) for details.

## Conclusion Job Concurrency

The `conclusion` job — which handles reporting and post-agent cleanup — automatically receives a workflow-specific concurrency group derived from the workflow filename:

```yaml wrap
conclusion:
  concurrency:
    group: "gh-aw-conclusion-my-workflow"
    cancel-in-progress: false
```

This prevents conclusion jobs from colliding when multiple agents run the same workflow concurrently. The group uses `cancel-in-progress: false` so queued conclusion runs complete in order rather than being discarded.

This concurrency group is set automatically during compilation and requires no manual configuration.

## Fan-Out Concurrency (`job-discriminator`)

When multiple workflow instances are dispatched concurrently with different inputs (fan-out pattern), compiler-generated job-level concurrency groups are static across all runs — causing all but the latest dispatched run to be cancelled as they compete for the same slot.

Use `concurrency.job-discriminator` to append a unique expression to compiler-generated job-level concurrency groups (`agent` and `output` jobs), making each dispatched run's group distinct:

```yaml wrap
concurrency:
  job-discriminator: ${{ inputs.finding_id }}
```

This generates a unique job-level concurrency group per dispatched run, preventing fan-out cancellations while preserving the per-workflow concurrency group at the workflow level.

Example usage:

```yaml wrap
concurrency:
  job-discriminator: ${{ inputs.finding_id }}
```

Common expressions:

| Scenario | Expression |
|---|---|
| Fan-out by a specific input | `${{ inputs.finding_id }}` |
| Universal uniqueness (e.g. scheduled runs) | `${{ github.run_id }}` |
| Dispatched or scheduled fallback | `${{ inputs.organization \|\| github.run_id }}` |

:::note
`job-discriminator` is a gh-aw extension and is stripped from the compiled lock file. It does not appear in the generated GitHub Actions YAML.
:::

:::note
`job-discriminator` has no effect on workflows triggered by `workflow_dispatch`-only, `push`, or `pull_request` events, or when the engine provides an explicit job-level concurrency configuration.
:::

## Related Documentation

- [AI Engines](/gh-aw/reference/engines/) - Engine configuration and capabilities
- [Frontmatter](/gh-aw/reference/frontmatter/) - Complete frontmatter reference
- [Workflow Structure](/gh-aw/reference/workflow-structure/) - Overall workflow organization
- [Safe Outputs](/gh-aw/reference/safe-outputs/) - Safe output processing and job configuration

1	package workflow
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"slices"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/constants"
10		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
11	)
12	
13	var notifyCommentLog = logger.New("workflow:notify_comment")
14	
15	// buildConclusionJob creates a job that handles workflow completion tasks
16	// This job is generated when safe-outputs are configured and handles:
17	// - Updating status comments (if status-comment: true)
18	// - Processing noop messages
19	// - Handling agent failures
20	// - Recording missing tools
21	// This job runs when:
22	// 1. always() - runs even if agent fails
23	// 2. Agent job was not skipped
24	// 3. NO add_comment output was produced by the agent (avoids duplicate updates)
25	// This job depends on all safe output jobs to ensure it runs last
26	func (c *Compiler) buildConclusionJob(data *WorkflowData, mainJobName string, safeOutputJobNames []string) (*Job, error) {
27		notifyCommentLog.Printf("Building conclusion job: main_job=%s, safe_output_jobs_count=%d", mainJobName, len(safeOutputJobNames))
28	
29		// Always create this job when safe-outputs exist (because noop is always enabled)
30		// This ensures noop messages can be handled even without reactions
31		if data.SafeOutputs == nil {
32			notifyCommentLog.Printf("Skipping job: no safe-outputs configured")
33			return nil, nil // No safe-outputs configured, no need for conclusion job
34		}
35	
36		// Build the job steps
37		var steps []string
38	
39		// Add setup step to copy scripts
40		setupActionRef := c.resolveActionReference("./actions/setup", data)
41		if setupActionRef != "" || c.actionMode.IsScript() {
42			// For dev mode (local action path), checkout the actions folder first
43			steps = append(steps, c.generateCheckoutActionsFolder(data)...)
44	
45			// Notify comment job doesn't need project support
46			steps = append(steps, c.generateSetupStep(setupActionRef, SetupActionDestination, false)...)
47		}
48	
49		// Add GitHub App token minting step if app is configured
50		if data.SafeOutputs.GitHubApp != nil {
51			// Compute permissions based on configured safe outputs (principle of least privilege)
52			permissions := ComputePermissionsForSafeOutputs(data.SafeOutputs)
53			// For workflow_call relay workflows, scope the token to the platform repo name only
54			// (not the full slug) because actions/create-github-app-token expects repo names
55			// without the owner prefix when `owner` is also set.
56			var appTokenFallbackRepo string
57			if hasWorkflowCallTrigger(data.On) {
58				appTokenFallbackRepo = "${{ needs.activation.outputs.target_repo_name }}"
59			}
60			steps = append(steps, c.buildGitHubAppTokenMintStep(data.SafeOutputs.GitHubApp, permissions, appTokenFallbackRepo)...)
61		}
62	
63		// Add artifact download steps once (shared by noop and conclusion steps).
64		// In workflow_call context, use the per-invocation prefix to avoid artifact name clashes.
65		steps = append(steps, buildAgentOutputDownloadSteps(artifactPrefixExprForDownstreamJob(data))...)
66	
67		// Add noop processing step if noop is configured
68		if data.SafeOutputs.NoOp != nil {
69			// Build custom environment variables specific to noop
70			var noopEnvVars []string
71			noopEnvVars = append(noopEnvVars, buildTemplatableIntEnvVar("GH_AW_NOOP_MAX", data.SafeOutputs.NoOp.Max)...)
72	
73			// Add workflow metadata for consistency
74			noopEnvVars = append(noopEnvVars, buildWorkflowMetadataEnvVarsWithTrackerID(data.Name, data.Source, data.TrackerID)...)
75	
76			// Build the noop processing step (without artifact downloads - already added above)
77			noopSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
78				StepName:      "Process No-Op Messages",
79				StepID:        "noop",
80				MainJobName:   mainJobName,
81				CustomEnvVars: noopEnvVars,
82				Script:        getNoOpScript(),
83				ScriptFile:    "noop.cjs",
84				CustomToken:   data.SafeOutputs.NoOp.GitHubToken,
85			})
86			steps = append(steps, noopSteps...)
87		}
88	
89		// Add missing_tool processing step if missing-tool is configured
90		if data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool != nil {
91			// Build custom environment variables specific to missing-tool
92			var missingToolEnvVars []string
93			missingToolEnvVars = append(missingToolEnvVars, buildTemplatableIntEnvVar("GH_AW_MISSING_TOOL_MAX", data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.Max)...)
94	
95			// Add create-issue configuration
96			if data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.CreateIssue {
97				missingToolEnvVars = append(missingToolEnvVars, "          GH_AW_MISSING_TOOL_CREATE_ISSUE: \"true\"\n")
98			}
99	
100			// Add title-prefix configuration
101			if data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.TitlePrefix != "" {
102				missingToolEnvVars = append(missingToolEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_MISSING_TOOL_TITLE_PREFIX: %q\n", data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.TitlePrefix))
103			}
104	
105			// Add labels configuration
106			if len(data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.Labels) > 0 {
107				labelsJSON, err := json.Marshal(data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.Labels)
108				if err == nil {
109					missingToolEnvVars = append(missingToolEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_MISSING_TOOL_LABELS: %q\n", string(labelsJSON)))
110				}
111			}
112	
113			// Add workflow metadata for consistency
114			missingToolEnvVars = append(missingToolEnvVars, buildWorkflowMetadataEnvVarsWithTrackerID(data.Name, data.Source, data.TrackerID)...)
115	
116			// Build the missing_tool processing step (without artifact downloads - already added above)
117			missingToolSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
118				StepName:      "Record Missing Tool",
119				StepID:        "missing_tool",
120				MainJobName:   mainJobName,
121				CustomEnvVars: missingToolEnvVars,
122				Script:        "const { main } = require('${{ runner.temp }}/gh-aw/actions/missing_tool.cjs'); await main();",
123				ScriptFile:    "missing_tool.cjs",
124				CustomToken:   data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool.GitHubToken,
125			})
126			steps = append(steps, missingToolSteps...)
127		}
128	
129		// Add agent failure handling step - creates/updates an issue when agent job fails
130		// This step always runs and checks if the agent job failed
131		// Build environment variables for the agent failure handler
132		var agentFailureEnvVars []string
133		agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, buildWorkflowMetadataEnvVarsWithTrackerID(data.Name, data.Source, data.TrackerID)...)
134		agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\n")
135		agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_AGENT_CONCLUSION: ${{ needs.%s.result }}\n", mainJobName))
136		agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_WORKFLOW_ID: %q\n", data.WorkflowID))
137	
138		// Pass the engine ID so the failure handler can surface which AI engine terminated
139		if data.EngineConfig != nil && data.EngineConfig.ID != "" {
140			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_ENGINE_ID: %q\n", data.EngineConfig.ID))
141		}
142	
143		// Only add secret_verification_result if the engine provides a validate-secret step.
144		// The validate-secret step runs in the activation job, so the output is on needs.activation.
145		engine, err := c.getAgenticEngine(data.AI)
146		if err != nil {
147			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get agentic engine: %w", err)
148		}
149		if EngineHasValidateSecretStep(engine, data) {
150			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_SECRET_VERIFICATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.secret_verification_result }}\n", string(constants.ActivationJobName)))
151		}
152	
153		// Add checkout_pr_success to detect PR checkout failures (e.g., PR merged and branch deleted)
154		// Only add if the checkout-pr step will be generated (requires contents read access)
155		if ShouldGeneratePRCheckoutStep(data) {
156			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_CHECKOUT_PR_SUCCESS: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.checkout_pr_success }}\n", mainJobName))
157		}
158	
159		// Pass inference access error output for Copilot engine
160		// This detects when the Copilot CLI fails due to the token lacking inference access
161		if _, ok := engine.(*CopilotEngine); ok {
162			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_INFERENCE_ACCESS_ERROR: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.inference_access_error }}\n", mainJobName))
163		}
164	
165		// Pass assignment error outputs from safe_outputs job if assign-to-agent is configured
166		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.AssignToAgent != nil {
167			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_ASSIGNMENT_ERRORS: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.assign_to_agent_assignment_errors }}\n")
168			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_ASSIGNMENT_ERROR_COUNT: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.assign_to_agent_assignment_error_count }}\n")
169		}
170	
171		// Pass copilot assignment failure outputs from safe_outputs job if create-issue with copilot assignee is configured
172		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.CreateIssues != nil && hasCopilotAssignee(data.SafeOutputs.CreateIssues.Assignees) {
173			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_ASSIGN_COPILOT_FAILURE_COUNT: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.assign_copilot_failure_count }}\n")
174			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_ASSIGN_COPILOT_ERRORS: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.assign_copilot_errors }}\n")
175		}
176	
177		// Pass create_discussion error outputs from safe_outputs job if create-discussions is configured
178		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.CreateDiscussions != nil {
179			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_CREATE_DISCUSSION_ERRORS: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.create_discussion_errors }}\n")
180			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_CREATE_DISCUSSION_ERROR_COUNT: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.create_discussion_error_count }}\n")
181		}
182	
183		// Pass code-push failure outputs from safe_outputs job if push-to-pull-request-branch or create-pull-request is configured
184		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && (data.SafeOutputs.PushToPullRequestBranch != nil || data.SafeOutputs.CreatePullRequests != nil) {
185			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_CODE_PUSH_FAILURE_ERRORS: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.code_push_failure_errors }}\n")
186			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_CODE_PUSH_FAILURE_COUNT: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.code_push_failure_count }}\n")
187		}
188	
189		// Pass GitHub App token minting failure status so the handler can surface auth errors.
190		// The safe_outputs job tracks whether its token step failed as a job output.
191		// The conclusion job tracks its own app token step outcome directly via steps context.
192		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.GitHubApp != nil {
193			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUTS_APP_TOKEN_MINTING_FAILED: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.app_token_minting_failed }}\n")
194			// Also check the conclusion job's own app token step outcome; this is important because
195			// the Handle Agent Failure step must use if: always() to run even when this step fails.
196			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_CONCLUSION_APP_TOKEN_MINTING_FAILED: ${{ steps.safe-outputs-app-token.outcome == 'failure' }}\n")
197		}
198	
199		// Pass activation job GitHub App token minting failure status if configured.
200		if data.ActivationGitHubApp != nil {
201			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_ACTIVATION_APP_TOKEN_MINTING_FAILED: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.activation_app_token_minting_failed }}\n", string(constants.ActivationJobName)))
202		}
203	
204		// Always pass lockdown check failure status so the handler can surface configuration
205		// errors even when the agent job was skipped due to the lockdown check failing.
206		agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_LOCKDOWN_CHECK_FAILED: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.lockdown_check_failed }}\n", string(constants.ActivationJobName)))
207	
208		// Pass custom messages config if present
209		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.Messages != nil {
210			messagesJSON, err := serializeMessagesConfig(data.SafeOutputs.Messages)
211			if err != nil {
212				notifyCommentLog.Printf("Warning: failed to serialize messages config for agent failure handler: %v", err)
213			} else if messagesJSON != "" {
214				agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUT_MESSAGES: %q\n", messagesJSON))
215			}
216		}
217	
218		// Pass repo-memory failure outputs if repo-memory is configured
219		// This allows the agent failure handler to report both job-level failures and validation issues
220		if data.RepoMemoryConfig != nil && len(data.RepoMemoryConfig.Memories) > 0 {
221			// Pass the overall push_repo_memory job result so the failure handler
222			// can report when the push job itself fails (e.g. permission or configuration errors)
223			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_PUSH_REPO_MEMORY_RESULT: ${{ needs.push_repo_memory.result }}\n")
224			for _, memory := range data.RepoMemoryConfig.Memories {
225				// Add validation status for each memory
226				agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_REPO_MEMORY_VALIDATION_FAILED_%s: ${{ needs.push_repo_memory.outputs.validation_failed_%s }}\n", memory.ID, memory.ID))
227				agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_REPO_MEMORY_VALIDATION_ERROR_%s: ${{ needs.push_repo_memory.outputs.validation_error_%s }}\n", memory.ID, memory.ID))
228				agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_REPO_MEMORY_PATCH_SIZE_EXCEEDED_%s: ${{ needs.push_repo_memory.outputs.patch_size_exceeded_%s }}\n", memory.ID, memory.ID))
229			}
230		}
231	
232		// Pass group-reports configuration flag (defaults to false if not specified)
233		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.GroupReports {
234			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_GROUP_REPORTS: \"true\"\n")
235		} else {
236			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_GROUP_REPORTS: \"false\"\n")
237		}
238	
239		// Pass report-failure-as-issue configuration flag (defaults to true if not specified)
240		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.ReportFailureAsIssue != nil && !*data.SafeOutputs.ReportFailureAsIssue {
241			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_FAILURE_REPORT_AS_ISSUE: \"false\"\n")
242		} else {
243			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, "          GH_AW_FAILURE_REPORT_AS_ISSUE: \"true\"\n")
244		}
245	
246		// Pass failure-issue-repo configuration (optional, defaults to current repo)
247		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.FailureIssueRepo != "" {
248			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_FAILURE_ISSUE_REPO: %q\n", data.SafeOutputs.FailureIssueRepo))
249		}
250	
251		// Pass timeout minutes value so the failure handler can provide an actionable hint when timed out
252		timeoutValue := strings.TrimPrefix(data.TimeoutMinutes, "timeout-minutes: ")
253		if timeoutValue != "" {
254			agentFailureEnvVars = append(agentFailureEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_TIMEOUT_MINUTES: %q\n", timeoutValue))
255		}
256	
257		// Build the agent failure handling step.
258		// Use if: always() so this step runs even when an earlier step in the conclusion job
259		// (such as the GitHub App token minting step) has failed. The handler uses the default
260		// GITHUB_TOKEN and does not depend on the app-minted token.
261		agentFailureSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
262			StepName:      "Handle Agent Failure",
263			StepID:        "handle_agent_failure",
264			MainJobName:   mainJobName,
265			CustomEnvVars: agentFailureEnvVars,
266			Script:        "const { main } = require('${{ runner.temp }}/gh-aw/actions/handle_agent_failure.cjs'); await main();",
267			ScriptFile:    "handle_agent_failure.cjs",
268			CustomToken:   "", // Will use default GITHUB_TOKEN
269			StepCondition: "always()",
270		})
271		steps = append(steps, agentFailureSteps...)
272	
273		// Add noop message handling step - posts noop messages to the "agent runs" issue
274		// This step runs when the agent succeeded with only noop outputs (no other safe-outputs)
275		// Build environment variables for the noop message handler
276		var noopMessageEnvVars []string
277		noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, buildWorkflowMetadataEnvVarsWithTrackerID(data.Name, data.Source, data.TrackerID)...)
278		noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, "          GH_AW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\n")
279		// Pass the agent conclusion to check if the agent succeeded
280		noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_AGENT_CONCLUSION: ${{ needs.%s.result }}\n", mainJobName))
281		// Pass the noop message from the noop processing step
282		if data.SafeOutputs.NoOp != nil {
283			noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, "          GH_AW_NOOP_MESSAGE: ${{ steps.noop.outputs.noop_message }}\n")
284			// Pass the report-as-issue configuration
285			noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, buildTemplatableBoolEnvVar("GH_AW_NOOP_REPORT_AS_ISSUE", data.SafeOutputs.NoOp.ReportAsIssue)...)
286			if data.SafeOutputs.NoOp.ReportAsIssue == nil {
287				noopMessageEnvVars = append(noopMessageEnvVars, "          GH_AW_NOOP_REPORT_AS_ISSUE: \"true\"\n")
288			}
289		}
290	
291		// Build the noop message handling step
292		noopMessageSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
293			StepName:      "Handle No-Op Message",
294			StepID:        "handle_noop_message",
295			MainJobName:   mainJobName,
296			CustomEnvVars: noopMessageEnvVars,
297			Script:        "const { main } = require('${{ runner.temp }}/gh-aw/actions/handle_noop_message.cjs'); await main();",
298			ScriptFile:    "handle_noop_message.cjs",
299			CustomToken:   "", // Will use default GITHUB_TOKEN
300		})
301		steps = append(steps, noopMessageSteps...)
302	
303		// Add create_pull_request error handling step if create-pull-request is configured
304		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.CreatePullRequests != nil {
305			// Build environment variables for the create PR error handler
306			var createPRErrorEnvVars []string
307			// Note: With consolidated safe outputs, individual handler errors are not exposed as outputs.
308			// The error handler script will skip gracefully if CREATE_PR_ERROR_MESSAGE is not set.
309			createPRErrorEnvVars = append(createPRErrorEnvVars, buildWorkflowMetadataEnvVarsWithTrackerID(data.Name, data.Source, data.TrackerID)...)
310			createPRErrorEnvVars = append(createPRErrorEnvVars, "          GH_AW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\n")
311	
312			// Build the create PR error handling step
313			createPRErrorSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
314				StepName:      "Handle Create Pull Request Error",
315				StepID:        "handle_create_pr_error",
316				MainJobName:   mainJobName,
317				CustomEnvVars: createPRErrorEnvVars,
318				Script:        "const { main } = require('${{ runner.temp }}/gh-aw/actions/handle_create_pr_error.cjs'); await main();",
319				ScriptFile:    "handle_create_pr_error.cjs",
320				CustomToken:   "", // Will use default GITHUB_TOKEN
321			})
322			steps = append(steps, createPRErrorSteps...)
323		}
324	
325		// Build environment variables for the conclusion script
326		var customEnvVars []string
327		customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_COMMENT_ID: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.comment_id }}\n", constants.ActivationJobName))
328		customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_COMMENT_REPO: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.comment_repo }}\n", constants.ActivationJobName))
329		customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, "          GH_AW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\n")
330		customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_WORKFLOW_NAME: %q\n", data.Name))
331		// Pass the tracker-id if present
332		if data.TrackerID != "" {
333			customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_TRACKER_ID: %q\n", data.TrackerID))
334		}
335		customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_AGENT_CONCLUSION: ${{ needs.%s.result }}\n", mainJobName))
336	
337		// Pass detection conclusion if threat detection is enabled (in separate detection job)
338		if IsDetectionJobEnabled(data.SafeOutputs) {
339			customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_DETECTION_CONCLUSION: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.detection_conclusion }}\n", constants.DetectionJobName))
340			notifyCommentLog.Print("Added detection conclusion environment variable to conclusion job")
341		}
342	
343		// Pass assignment error count to the conclusion step so the status comment reflects assignment failures
344		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.AssignToAgent != nil {
345			customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, "          GH_AW_ASSIGNMENT_ERROR_COUNT: ${{ needs.safe_outputs.outputs.assign_to_agent_assignment_error_count }}\n")
346		}
347	
348		// Pass custom messages config if present
349		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.Messages != nil {
350			messagesJSON, err := serializeMessagesConfig(data.SafeOutputs.Messages)
351			if err != nil {
352				notifyCommentLog.Printf("Warning: failed to serialize messages config: %v", err)
353			} else if messagesJSON != "" {
354				customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUT_MESSAGES: %q\n", messagesJSON))
355			}
356		}
357	
358		// Pass safe output job information for link generation
359		if len(safeOutputJobNames) > 0 {
360			safeOutputJobsJSON, jobURLEnvVars := buildSafeOutputJobsEnvVars(safeOutputJobNames)
361			if safeOutputJobsJSON != "" {
362				customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, fmt.Sprintf("          GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUT_JOBS: %q\n", safeOutputJobsJSON))
363				customEnvVars = append(customEnvVars, jobURLEnvVars...)
364				notifyCommentLog.Printf("Added safe output jobs info for %d job(s)", len(safeOutputJobNames))
365			}
366		}
367	
368		// Get token from config
369		var token string
370		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && data.SafeOutputs.AddComments != nil {
371			token = data.SafeOutputs.AddComments.GitHubToken
372		}
373	
374		// Only add the conclusion update step if status comments are explicitly enabled
375		if data.StatusComment != nil && *data.StatusComment {
376			// Build the conclusion GitHub Script step (without artifact downloads - already added above)
377			scriptSteps := c.buildGitHubScriptStepWithoutDownload(data, GitHubScriptStepConfig{
378				StepName:      "Update reaction comment with completion status",
379				StepID:        "conclusion",
380				MainJobName:   mainJobName,
381				CustomEnvVars: customEnvVars,
382				Script:        getNotifyCommentErrorScript(),
383				ScriptFile:    "notify_comment_error.cjs",
384				CustomToken:   token,
385			})
386			steps = append(steps, scriptSteps...)
387		}
388	
389		// Note: Unlock step has been moved to a dedicated unlock job
390		// that always runs, even if this conclusion job doesn't run.
391		// See buildUnlockJob() in compiler_unlock_job.go
392	
393		// Add GitHub App token invalidation step if app is configured
394		if data.SafeOutputs.GitHubApp != nil {
395			notifyCommentLog.Print("Adding GitHub App token invalidation step to conclusion job")
396			steps = append(steps, c.buildGitHubAppTokenInvalidationStep()...)
397		}
398	
399		// Build the condition for this job:
400		// 1. always() - run even if agent fails
401		// 2. agent was activated (not skipped) OR lockdown check failed in activation job
402		// 3. IF comment_id exists: add_comment job either doesn't exist OR hasn't created a comment yet
403		//
404		// Note: The job should always run to handle noop messages (either update comment or write to summary)
405		// The script (notify_comment_error.cjs) handles the case where there's no comment gracefully
406	
407		alwaysFunc := BuildFunctionCall("always")
408	
409		// Check that agent job was activated (not skipped)
410		agentNotSkipped := BuildNotEquals(
411			BuildPropertyAccess(fmt.Sprintf("needs.%s.result", constants.AgentJobName)),
412			BuildStringLiteral("skipped"),
413		)
414	
415		// Check if the lockdown check failed in the activation job — when this happens the agent
416		// is skipped, but we still want the conclusion job to run so it can report the failure.
417		lockdownCheckFailed := BuildEquals(
418			BuildPropertyAccess(fmt.Sprintf("needs.%s.outputs.lockdown_check_failed", string(constants.ActivationJobName))),
419			BuildStringLiteral("true"),
420		)
421	
422		// Agent not skipped OR lockdown check failed
423		agentNotSkippedOrLockdownFailed := BuildOr(agentNotSkipped, lockdownCheckFailed)
424	
425		// Check if add_comment job exists in the safe output jobs
426		hasAddCommentJob := slices.Contains(safeOutputJobNames, "add_comment")
427	
428		// Build the condition based on whether add_comment job exists
429		var condition ConditionNode
430		if hasAddCommentJob {
431			// If add_comment job exists, also check that it hasn't already created a comment
432			// This prevents duplicate updates when add_comment has already updated the activation comment
433			noAddCommentOutput := &NotNode{
434				Child: BuildPropertyAccess("needs.add_comment.outputs.comment_id"),
435			}
436			condition = BuildAnd(
437				BuildAnd(alwaysFunc, agentNotSkippedOrLockdownFailed),
438				noAddCommentOutput,
439			)
440		} else {
441			// If add_comment job doesn't exist, just check the basic conditions
442			condition = BuildAnd(alwaysFunc, agentNotSkippedOrLockdownFailed)
443		}
444	
445		// Build dependencies - this job depends on all safe output jobs to ensure it runs last
446		needs := []string{mainJobName, string(constants.ActivationJobName)}
447		needs = append(needs, safeOutputJobNames...)
448	
449		// When threat detection is enabled, the conclusion job also depends on the detection job
450		// so that needs.detection.outputs.detection_conclusion is accessible.
451		if IsDetectionJobEnabled(data.SafeOutputs) {
452			needs = append(needs, string(constants.DetectionJobName))
453			notifyCommentLog.Print("Added detection job dependency to conclusion job")
454		}
455	
456		notifyCommentLog.Printf("Job built successfully: dependencies_count=%d", len(needs))
457	
458		// Create outputs for the job (include noop and missing_tool outputs if configured)
459		outputs := map[string]string{}
460		if data.SafeOutputs.NoOp != nil {
461			outputs["noop_message"] = "${{ steps.noop.outputs.noop_message }}"
462		}
463		if data.SafeOutputs.MissingTool != nil {
464			outputs["tools_reported"] = "${{ steps.missing_tool.outputs.tools_reported }}"
465			outputs["total_count"] = "${{ steps.missing_tool.outputs.total_count }}"
466		}
467	
468		// Compute permissions based on configured safe outputs (principle of least privilege)
469		permissions := ComputePermissionsForSafeOutputs(data.SafeOutputs)
470	
471		// Build concurrency config for the conclusion job using the workflow ID.
472		// This prevents concurrent agents on the same workflow from interfering with each other.
473		var concurrency string
474		if data.WorkflowID != "" {
475			group := "gh-aw-conclusion-" + data.WorkflowID
476			concurrency = c.indentYAMLLines(fmt.Sprintf("concurrency:\n  group: %q\n  cancel-in-progress: false", group), "    ")
477			notifyCommentLog.Printf("Configuring conclusion job concurrency group: %s", group)
478		}
479	
480		job := &Job{
481			Name:        "conclusion",
482			If:          RenderCondition(condition),
483			RunsOn:      c.formatFrameworkJobRunsOn(data),
484			Environment: c.indentYAMLLines(resolveSafeOutputsEnvironment(data), "    "),
485			Permissions: permissions.RenderToYAML(),
486			Concurrency: concurrency,
487			Steps:       steps,
488			Needs:       needs,
489			Outputs:     outputs,
490		}
491	
492		return job, nil
493	}
494	
495	// systemSafeOutputJobNames contains job names that are built-in system jobs and should not be
496	// treated as custom safe output job types in the GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUT_JOBS mapping.
497	// The safe output handler manager uses this mapping to determine which message types are
498	// handled by custom job steps (and therefore should be silently skipped rather than flagged
499	// as "no handler loaded").
500	var systemSafeOutputJobNames = map[string]bool{
501		"safe_outputs":  true, // consolidated safe outputs job
502		"upload_assets": true, // upload assets job
503	}
504	
505	// buildSafeOutputJobsEnvVars creates environment variables for safe output job URLs
506	// Returns both a JSON mapping and the actual environment variable declarations.
507	// The mapping includes:
508	//   - Built-in jobs with known URL outputs (e.g., create_issue → issue_url)
509	//   - Custom safe-output jobs (from safe-outputs.jobs) with an empty URL key, so the handler
510	//     manager knows those message types are handled by a dedicated job step and should be
511	//     skipped gracefully rather than reported as "No handler loaded".
512	func buildSafeOutputJobsEnvVars(jobNames []string) (string, []string) {
513		// Map job names to their expected URL output keys
514		jobOutputMapping := make(map[string]string)
515		var envVars []string
516	
517		for _, jobName := range jobNames {
518			var urlKey string
519			switch jobName {
520			case "create_issue":
521				urlKey = "issue_url"
522			case "add_comment":
523				urlKey = "comment_url"
524			case "create_pull_request":
525				urlKey = "pull_request_url"
526			case "create_discussion":
527				urlKey = "discussion_url"
528			case "create_pr_review_comment":
529				urlKey = "review_comment_url"
530			case "close_issue":
531				urlKey = "issue_url"
532			case "close_pull_request":
533				urlKey = "pull_request_url"
534			case "close_discussion":
535				urlKey = "discussion_url"
536			case "create_agent_session":
537				urlKey = "task_url"
538			case "push_to_pull_request_branch":
539				urlKey = "commit_url"
540			default:
541				if !systemSafeOutputJobNames[jobName] {
542					// Custom safe-output job: include in the mapping with an empty URL key so the
543					// handler manager can silently skip messages of this type.
544					jobOutputMapping[jobName] = ""
545				}
546				continue
547			}
548	
549			jobOutputMapping[jobName] = urlKey
550	
551			// Add environment variable for this job's URL output
552			envVarName := fmt.Sprintf("GH_AW_OUTPUT_%s_%s",
553				toEnvVarCase(jobName),
554				toEnvVarCase(urlKey))
555			envVars = append(envVars,
556				fmt.Sprintf("          %s: ${{ needs.%s.outputs.%s }}\n",
557					envVarName, jobName, urlKey))
558		}
559	
560		if len(jobOutputMapping) == 0 {
561			return "", nil
562		}
563	
564		jsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(jobOutputMapping)
565		if err != nil {
566			notifyCommentLog.Printf("Warning: failed to marshal safe output jobs info: %v", err)
567			return "", nil
568		}
569	
570		return string(jsonBytes), envVars
571	}
572	
573	// toEnvVarCase converts a string to uppercase environment variable case
574	func toEnvVarCase(s string) string {
575		// Convert to uppercase and keep underscores
576		var result strings.Builder
577		for _, ch := range s {
578			if ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z' {
579				result.WriteRune(ch - 32) // Convert to uppercase
580			} else if ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z' {
581				result.WriteRune(ch)
582			} else if ch == '_' {
583				result.WriteString("_")
584			}
585		}
586		return result.String()
587	}

12	// CompilerOption is a functional option for configuring a Compiler
13	type CompilerOption func(*Compiler)
14	
15	// WithVerbose sets the verbose logging flag
16	func WithVerbose(verbose bool) CompilerOption {
17		return func(c *Compiler) { c.verbose = verbose }
18	}
19	
20	// WithEngineOverride sets the AI engine override
21	func WithEngineOverride(engine string) CompilerOption {
22		return func(c *Compiler) { c.engineOverride = engine }
23	}
24	
25	// WithCustomOutput sets a custom output path for the compiled workflow
26	func WithCustomOutput(path string) CompilerOption {
27		return func(c *Compiler) { c.customOutput = path }
28	}
29	
30	// WithSkipValidation configures whether to skip schema validation
31	func WithSkipValidation(skip bool) CompilerOption {
32		return func(c *Compiler) { c.skipValidation = skip }
33	}
34	
35	// WithNoEmit configures whether to validate without generating lock files
36	func WithNoEmit(noEmit bool) CompilerOption {
37		return func(c *Compiler) { c.noEmit = noEmit }
38	}
39	
40	// WithFailFast configures whether to stop at first validation error
41	func WithFailFast(failFast bool) CompilerOption {
42		return func(c *Compiler) { c.failFast = failFast }
43	}
44	
45	// WithWorkflowIdentifier sets the identifier for the current workflow being compiled
46	func WithWorkflowIdentifier(identifier string) CompilerOption {
47		return func(c *Compiler) { c.workflowIdentifier = identifier }
48	}

50	// FileTracker interface for tracking files created during compilation
51	type FileTracker interface {
52		TrackCreated(filePath string)
53	}

55	// Compiler handles converting markdown workflows to GitHub Actions YAML
56	type Compiler struct {
57		verbose                 bool
58		quiet                   bool // If true, suppress success messages (for interactive mode)
59		engineOverride          string
60		customOutput            string              // If set, output will be written to this path instead of default location
61		version                 string              // Version of the extension
62		skipValidation          bool                // If true, skip schema validation
63		noEmit                  bool                // If true, validate without generating lock files
64		strictMode              bool                // If true, enforce strict validation requirements
65		trialMode               bool                // If true, suppress safe outputs for trial mode execution
66		trialLogicalRepoSlug    string              // If set in trial mode, the logical repository to checkout
67		refreshStopTime         bool                // If true, regenerate stop-after times instead of preserving existing ones
68		forceRefreshActionPins  bool                // If true, clear action cache and resolve all actions from GitHub API
69		failFast                bool                // If true, stop at first validation error instead of collecting all errors
70		actionCacheCleared      bool                // Tracks if action cache has already been cleared (for forceRefreshActionPins)
71		markdownPath            string              // Path to the markdown file being compiled (for context in dynamic tool generation)
72		actionMode              ActionMode          // Mode for generating JavaScript steps (inline vs custom actions)
73		actionTag               string              // Override action SHA or tag for actions/setup (when set, overrides actionMode to release)
74		actionsRepo             string              // Override the external actions repository (default: github/gh-aw-actions)
75		jobManager              *JobManager         // Manages jobs and dependencies
76		engineRegistry          *EngineRegistry     // Registry of available agentic engines
77		engineCatalog           *EngineCatalog      // Catalog of engine definitions backed by the registry
78		fileTracker             FileTracker         // Optional file tracker for tracking created files
79		warningCount            int                 // Number of warnings encountered during compilation
80		stepOrderTracker        *StepOrderTracker   // Tracks step ordering for validation
81		actionCache             *ActionCache        // Shared cache for action pin resolutions across all workflows
82		actionResolver          *ActionResolver     // Shared resolver for action pins across all workflows
83		actionPinWarnings       map[string]bool     // Shared cache of already-warned action pin failures (key: "repo@version")
84		importCache             *parser.ImportCache // Shared cache for imported workflow files
85		workflowIdentifier      string              // Identifier for the current workflow being compiled (for schedule scattering)
86		scheduleWarnings        []string            // Accumulated schedule warnings for this compiler instance
87		repositorySlug          string              // Repository slug (owner/repo) used as seed for scattering
88		artifactManager         *ArtifactManager    // Tracks artifact uploads/downloads for validation
89		scheduleFriendlyFormats map[int]string      // Maps schedule item index to friendly format string for current workflow
90		gitRoot                 string              // Git repository root directory (if set, used for action cache path)
91		contentOverride         string              // If set, use this content instead of reading from disk (for Wasm/in-memory compilation)
92		skipHeader              bool                // If true, skip ASCII art header in generated YAML (for Wasm/editor mode)
93		inlinePrompt            bool                // If true, inline markdown content in YAML instead of using runtime-import macros (for Wasm builds)
94	}

96	// NewCompiler creates a new workflow compiler with functional options.
97	// By default, it auto-detects the version and action mode.
98	// Common options: WithVerbose, WithEngineOverride, WithNoEmit, WithSkipValidation
99	func NewCompiler(opts ...CompilerOption) *Compiler {
100		// Get the current compiler version (set by SetVersion during CLI initialization)
101		version := GetVersion()
102	
103		// Auto-detect git repository root for action cache path resolution
104		// This ensures actions-lock.json is created at repo root regardless of CWD
105		gitRoot := findGitRoot()
106	
107		// Create compiler with defaults
108		c := &Compiler{
109			verbose:           false,
110			engineOverride:    "",
111			version:           version,
112			skipValidation:    true,                      // Skip validation by default for now since existing workflows don't fully comply
113			actionMode:        DetectActionMode(version), // Auto-detect action mode based on version
114			jobManager:        NewJobManager(),
115			engineRegistry:    GetGlobalEngineRegistry(),
116			engineCatalog:     NewEngineCatalog(GetGlobalEngineRegistry()),
117			stepOrderTracker:  NewStepOrderTracker(),
118			artifactManager:   NewArtifactManager(),
119			actionPinWarnings: make(map[string]bool), // Initialize warning cache
120			gitRoot:           gitRoot,               // Auto-detected git root
121		}
122	
123		// Apply functional options
124		for _, opt := range opts {
125			opt(c)
126		}
127		// Auto-detect action mode based on version in case version has been update
128		c.actionMode = DetectActionMode(c.version)
129	
130		logTypes.Printf("Created compiler: version=%s, actionMode=%s, skipValidation=%t, strictMode=%t", c.version, c.actionMode, c.skipValidation, c.strictMode)
131	
132		return c
133	}

261	// getSharedActionResolver returns the shared action resolver, initializing it on first use
262	// This ensures all workflows compiled by this compiler instance share the same in-memory cache
263	func (c *Compiler) getSharedActionResolver() (*ActionCache, *ActionResolver) {
264		if c.actionCache == nil {
265			// Initialize cache and resolver on first use
266			// Use git root if provided, otherwise fall back to current working directory
267			baseDir := c.gitRoot
268			if baseDir == "" {
269				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
270				if err != nil {
271					cwd = "."
272				}
273				baseDir = cwd
274			}
275			c.actionCache = NewActionCache(baseDir)
276	
277			// Load existing cache unless force refresh is enabled
278			if !c.forceRefreshActionPins {
279				_ = c.actionCache.Load() // Ignore errors if cache doesn't exist
280			} else {
281				logTypes.Print("Force refresh action pins enabled: skipping cache load and will resolve all actions dynamically")
282				// Mark as cleared since we skipped loading
283				c.actionCacheCleared = true
284			}
285	
286			c.actionResolver = NewActionResolver(c.actionCache)
287			logTypes.Print("Initialized shared action cache and resolver for compiler")
288		} else if c.forceRefreshActionPins && !c.actionCacheCleared {
289			// If cache already exists but force refresh is set and we haven't cleared it yet, clear it once
290			logTypes.Print("Force refresh action pins: clearing existing cache once for this run")
291			c.actionCache.Entries = make(map[string]ActionCacheEntry)
292			c.actionCacheCleared = true
293		}
294		return c.actionCache, c.actionResolver
295	}
296	
297	// getSharedImportCache returns the shared import cache, initializing it on first use
298	// This ensures all workflows compiled by this compiler instance share the same import cache
299	func (c *Compiler) getSharedImportCache() *parser.ImportCache {
300		if c.importCache == nil {
301			// Initialize cache on first use
302			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
303			if err != nil {
304				cwd = "."
305			}
306			c.importCache = parser.NewImportCache(cwd)
307			logTypes.Print("Initialized shared import cache for compiler")
308		}
309		return c.importCache
310	}
311	
312	// GetSharedActionCache returns the shared action cache used by this compiler instance.
313	// The cache is lazily initialized on first access and shared across all workflows.
314	// This allows action SHA validation and other operations to reuse cached resolutions.
315	func (c *Compiler) GetSharedActionCache() *ActionCache {
316		cache, _ := c.getSharedActionResolver()
317		return cache
318	}

  # Whether to cancel in-progress workflows in the same concurrency group when a new
